title: Tilebox Launches Verifiable AI Workflows for Satellite Data
summary: Tilebox introduced infrastructure for verifiable AI workflows on Earth observation satellite data, enabling teams to use agents through governed and repeatable processes.

title: K2 Space, Rocket Lab win key supplier roles in Space Force satcom program
summary: K2 Space and Rocket Lab have been selected as key suppliers for spacecraft buses to SES and Viasat under the U.S. Space Force's next-generation protected satcom program. This collaboration aims to enhance military satellite communications capabilities.

title: GEO cancellations complicate space insurance recovery
summary: The cancellation of three geostationary satellites has created challenges for space insurers seeking financial recovery. SES disclosed the cancellations, impacting the industry's financial outlook.

title: Open Cosmos seeks deadline extension for broadband constellation
summary: Open Cosmos is requesting an extension for deploying its sovereign broadband constellation due to launch challenges. The British small satellite specialist faces delays in meeting the original timeline.

title: ESA awards contract for next-generation radar imaging satellites
summary: ESA awarded contracts to Thales Alenia Space and Airbus Defence and Space to build next-generation radar imaging satellites for Europe's Copernicus Earth observation program.

title: Long March 5 launches classified satellite, Zhuque-2E lofts direct-to-device test sats
summary: China launched a classified satellite using the Long March 5 rocket and deployed direct-to-device test satellites with the Zhuque-2E commercial launcher to enhance communications capabilities.

title: Missile defense in the age of saturation warfare
summary: The article discusses how modern military technologies have transformed warfare, shifting from direct battlefield confrontations to new forms of conflict. It highlights the challenges of missile defense in the era of saturation warfare.

title: Artemis 3 commander confident crew will be ready for 2027 mission
summary: The commander of NASA's Artemis 3 mission expressed confidence that the crew will be prepared for the mission, which is scheduled for 2027. The Artemis 3 mission is considered one of NASA's most complex undertakings.

title: House appropriators back $55.5 billion Space Force budget, omit reconciliation funds
summary: House appropriators approved a $55.5 billion budget for the Space Force but excluded $350 billion in proposed reconciliation funds, raising concerns for space programs like Golden Dome.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Space Supply Chain Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of Besxar and Charter Space as new members, expanding its space supply chain network with expertise in space-based semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Grab our best telescope for beginners with $100 off the Celestron NexStar 4SE
summary: Celestron's NexStar 4SE telescope, a beginner-friendly model with a 4-inch aperture and go-to technology, is on sale for $100 off on Amazon.

title: Astronauts watch from space as ice splinters from a glacier | Space photo of the day for June 11, 2026
summary: Astronauts observe ice splintering from a glacier in the Southern Patagonia Icefield from space. Changes in the icefield are visible in a space photo taken on June 11, 2026.

title: 'Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2' upgrades the sci-fi action shooter to the state-of-the-badass art
summary: Aliens: Fireteam Elite 2 is a sci-fi action shooter game that has been upgraded to a high level of quality. The preview was guided by game director Chris Cross.

title: Elon Musk wants to put 1 million AI satellites in space. Here's how SpaceX could do it
summary: Elon Musk plans to deploy 1 million AI satellites in space as part of SpaceX's strategy, with details emerging ahead of a potential IPO expected to make him a trillionaire.

title: Don't miss out on Star Trek: Strange New Worlds season 4 — stream it from anywhere at warp speed using this Norton VPN deal, now up to 70% off
summary: A Norton VPN advertisement promotes Star Trek: Strange New Worlds season 4 streaming with a 70% discount. The deal highlights Norton's online security and convenience.

title: James Webb Space Telescope finds evidence the mysterious 'little red dots' are black hole stars
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has found evidence suggesting that the mysterious 'little red dots' observed in the early universe may be black hole stars. This discovery could help explain the nature of these enigmatic cosmic objects.

title: NASA chief defends all-male Artemis 3 astronaut crew amid backlash: 'I don't think anyone should be reading into this'
summary: NASA's administrator defended the all-male Artemis 3 astronaut crew selection, stating the astronauts were chosen based on qualifications. The decision faced criticism and backlash from various groups.

title: 'Voltron: Legendary Defender' turns 10 today, and we think this mecha robot reboot was just as good as 'Power Rangers' and 'Transformers'
summary: The animated series 'Voltron: Legendary Defender' celebrates its 10th anniversary, praised for its quality alongside 'Power Rangers' and 'Transformers'. The reboot is noted for elevating the concept of mecha robots beyond typical expectations.

title: The asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs may have created a vast underground habitat for life that lasted 8 million years
summary: The asteroid impact that wiped out the dinosaurs may have created a vast underground habitat for life that lasted millions of years. This discovery highlights an unexpected ecological consequence of the catastrophic event.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit (LEO). These veteran astronauts will prepare for the upcoming crewed lunar landing.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: China, Rocket Lab, and SpaceX are scheduled to conduct multiple launches this week, including three Starlink missions. The flights will originate from China, California, Virginia, and Florida.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars after a six-month effort to restore communication. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions despite the loss of the New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is assessing damage from an anomaly and identifying future danger areas. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparations for the mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36 (LC-36) with the goal of returning the facility to flight status by the end of 2026.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news topic. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials capable of withstanding the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to ensure mission success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to enhance space exploration through training, operations, and public engagement. This technology is being used to create advanced simulation environments and improve mission support.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft parachute system, likely involving exposure to extreme conditions. This test is essential for ensuring safe landings during future missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life while adhering to planetary protection protocols.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox has launched an AI-focused update to enhance the effectiveness of AI agents as geospatial-data analysts. The update aims to improve their capabilities in processing and analyzing geospatial data.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on the accuracy of SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its potential IPO, though they agree the space industry is experiencing significant growth.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC Sat, entering the orbital data center market. The company aims to leverage space-based infrastructure for data processing and storage.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital secures $5 million in pre-seed funding to develop its ODC constellation, a network of smaller satellites designed to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground communication.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA confirmed that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lander for the Artemis III mission's Earth orbit test run in 2027, paving the way for future Moon landings. The event marked a key step in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace has selected Spanish startup Arkadia Space to supply a propulsion system for its 2027 satellite mission. This partnership highlights the growing collaboration between European space companies in advancing satellite technology.

title: ICEYE, Isar Aerospace Announce Significant Funding Rounds
summary: ICEYE, a Finnish Earth observation company, raised €450M ($520M) in a Series F funding round at a €10B ($12B) valuation. Isar Aerospace was also mentioned in the funding announcement.

title: Quantum Space To Go Public Via SPAC
summary: Quantum Space plans to go public through a SPAC merger to accelerate scaling and access capital. The company emphasizes the need for speed in its strategic growth plans.

title: NewOrbit Raises $18.5M to Build VLEO Spacecraft at Scale
summary: NewOrbit has raised $18.5 million to develop and scale Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) spacecraft. The company plans to launch its first VLEO-capable satellite, NEO-1, in 2028.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has come out of stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer. The company aims to build this network to support future space transportation and logistics.

title: Space Forge Secures £10 Million of New Investment through European, UK Space Agencies
summary: Space Forge secured £10 million ($13.4 million) in new investment from the European Space Agency and UK Space Agency. The funding is part of the ESA's General Support Technology Programme, supported by the UKSA.

title: Lithuania and Luxembourg Strengthen Space Defense Ties
summary: Lithuania and Luxembourg are strengthening their cooperation in space defense following a meeting between their respective defense ministers. The collaboration aims to enhance mutual security in space-related activities.

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation (EO) data company, is expanding into Brazil to leverage the country's climate pressures and rapid growth. The move marks a significant step in the company's global expansion strategy.

title: Airbus to Lead German Consortium for Sovereign ISR Capability
summary: Airbus has formed a German consortium to develop a sovereign intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) solution. The initiative was announced during the Berlin Air Show.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT to Purchase Avanti’s HYLAS 3 Satellite
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT will acquire Avanti's HYLAS 3 satellite currently in orbit. The satellite will undergo in-orbit testing following the purchase.

title: LeoLabs Deploys First Mobile Tracking Radar
summary: LeoLabs has deployed its first mobile space domain awareness tracking radar in the Asia-Pacific region, housed in a shipping container. The new system, called Scout-S, enhances space debris monitoring capabilities.

title: 40 People Who Shaped the Satellite Industry: Part One
summary: Via Satellite celebrates its 40-year anniversary in 2026 by highlighting 40 influential figures who shaped the satellite industry, both past and present. The feature is presented in a multi-part series.

title: Space Force Eyes Nearly $1.5B for Space Data Network Backbone in Future Reconciliation Bill
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to request nearly $1.5 billion for the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone in a future fiscal 2027 reconciliation bill. This funding aims to enhance the military's space-based data infrastructure.

title: Iceye’s Valuation Soars to 10 Billion Euro With New Raise
summary: Iceye's valuation surged to 10 billion euros ($12 billion) following a new funding round led by General Atlantic, which raised 450 million euros ($520 million).
